Caption: Sensitivity index analysis regions of EIS spectra. Analysed frequency bands are marked with H, M and L and, frequency limits separating each frequency region are marked with arrows.

Summary of the parameter sensitivities to EIS impedance frequency bands: H, M and L. Parameters are categorised into four groups according to sensitivities: H and M frequency bands (Charge-transfer region) sensitive, L frequency band (Warburg region) sensitive, sensitive to all-frequency bands (H, M and L), and, insensitive. The first bar (top most) in each parameter represent sensitivity to H frequency band, middle bar with M and bottom bar depicts the sensitivity of L band. Solid colour bars represent highest sensitivity due to real impedance and, hollow bars indicate imaginary impedances.

Caption: Flowchart of the multi-step, multi-SOC parameterisation framework.

Results of the numerical validation process for PSOg, MMPSOg and MMPSOd algorithms: (a) EIS model fitting comparisons, and (b) mean APEs of all algorithm-identified parameters grouped according to CT and Warburg sensitivities. The dash lines in each parameter group indicate the mean APEs of the respective group.

Experimental validation results: Parameterised P2D model performance with EIS simulations, Nyquist plots at (a) 100% SOC (b) 50% SOC (c) 20% SOC and, (d)–(f) depict Bode plots of imaginary components of (a)–(c) Nyquist, respectively. RMSE values of each simulation is given in (g).

Experimental validation results: Parameterised P2D model performance with voltage simulations, (a) 0.2C discharge with voltage relaxation (b) 1C pulse discharge with voltage relaxation, and (c) WLTP duty-cycle discharge. Below (a)–(c) show error between experimental and simulated voltage profiles. RMSE values for each test profile is given in (d) which also include segment-wise RMSE of CC segment and voltage relaxation segment of the 0.2C discharge test for better assessments.
